what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger entry that records an attachment (att-00136ab0) in the design-assets channel, declaring it as type images and pointing to the external image URI image://design-assets/icons/image (26) with an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
target is an image:// URI referencing 'design-assets/icons/image (26)'
The file body after the frontmatter is empty
standouts: The target URI contains literal spaces and parentheses, which is unusual for a clean identifier
It stores only metadata/pointer information, not the actual image data
The record is extremely minimal with no descriptive or provenance fields beyond the timestamp
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a kebab-case id prefixed by 'att-'
Follows a channel-memory ledger layout linking a channel to an external asset
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset
References an asset by URI rather than embedding it
concerns: The target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or linking issues
No MIME type, file size, dimensions, or checksum is recorded
No 'added_by' or provenance field is present
The human-readable '(26)' suffix in the target is unexplained
notes: A bare attachment pointer that registers an image asset under the design-assets channel with only an id, type, target URI, and timestamp.
questions: What is the actual file behind 'image://design-assets/icons/image (26)'?
Is 'image (26)' a generated/temporary filename or a canonical asset name?
Is 'images' a controlled attachment_type enum value?
Who or what process added this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated, but the fields match a catalog/pointer role: it records that an image attachment belongs to the design-assets channel and points to an external asset.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register and make discoverable a specific image asset attached to the design-assets channel, acting as a lightweight, referenceable ledger entry rather than a content container.
novelty: The target URI includes literal spaces and parentheses, and the record is unusually minimal for an asset attachment, lacking descriptive or integrity metadata.
contribution: It contributes a durable, timestamped pointer that maps an attachment identifier to a channel and an external image asset, enabling asset tracking and retrieval without storing binary content.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a channel-memory attachment ledger entry (att-00136ab0) for the design-assets channel, recording an images-type attachment added on 2026-06-22 that points to image://design-assets/icons/image (26).
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog and make discoverable a specific image asset attached to the design-assets channel.
